issues.title: TextFormField rebuilds whole widget on focus
issues.body:
## Flutter doctor
```
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[√] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.5.1, on Microsoft Windows [VersiÃ³n 10.0.19041.1237], locale es-MX)
[√] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 30.0.3)
[√] Android Studio (version 2020.3)
[√] VS Code (version 1.60.1)
[!] Connected device
    ! No devices available
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1.- You should have two screens, screen A and screen B.
2.- Screen A must be inside a TabBar with TickerProviderStateMixin
3.- They (A, B) must both be StatefulWidget.
4.- A is the main one, inside A this code is used to pass to B.
```
Navigator.push(context,
       PageRouteBuilder(
           opaque: false,
           pageBuilder: (context,
                  animation1,
                  animation2) => B(),
           transitionDuration:
           Duration(seconds: 0),
          ));
```
5.- Inside B, you should have a TextFormField without autofocus.

-- When I click inside TextFormField, screen B rebuilds 2 times causing problems with my code.
In flutter 2.2.3 the error did not happen.
In flutter 2.5.0 and 2.5.1 the error happen.

Expected results:
ScreenB should not be rebuilt.
In flutter 2.2.3 the widget is not rebuilt and should not be rebuilt as it only uses the processing resources of the cell phone.

Actual results:
Screen B is rebuilt 2 times.

I already tried many proposed solutions here on github, but none works for me .
1.- It does not matter if resizeToAvoidBottomInset is false or true.
2.- It doesn't matter whether or not I have this AutomaticKeepAliveClientMixin in my class.
3.- I don't have a GlobalKey.
4.- ¡IMPORTANT! If I put screen B as the main screen and click on the textformField, it does not rebuild (The error only happens when screen A is the main screen).


I have no logs because I used a breakpoint and the widget is rebuilt.
comments:
@Gcii Thanks for the code sample, I was able to reproduce the issue on `stable 2.5.0`, `2.5.1` and the 
`master, 2.6.0-6.0.pre.171` channel, On Navigating to next page and tapping on textfield the widget rebuilds. But I don't see it rebuilding on the `stable 2.2.3`, so this looks like a regression, I narrowed down your code and found that the issue is caused by nesting the MaterialApp.

<details>
<summary>code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/rendering.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: PageOne(),
    );
  }
}

class PageOne extends StatelessWidget {
  const PageOne({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
        // removing this MaterialApp, Screen C does not rebuild
        home: Stack(children: [
      Scaffold(
        resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false,
        appBar: AppBar(
          toolbarHeight: 0,
          elevation: 0,
          backgroundColor: Colors.teal[400],
        ),
        backgroundColor: Colors.white,
        body: ScreenB(),
      ),
    ]));
  }
}

class ScreenB extends StatefulWidget {
  @override
  _ScreenB createState() => _ScreenB();
}

class _ScreenB extends State<ScreenB> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    print('Screen B built');
    return Scaffold(
        backgroundColor: Colors.grey,
        appBar: PreferredSize(
            preferredSize: const Size.fromHeight(70.312),
            child: AppBar(
              title: const Text('Screen B'),
            )),
        body: Center(
          child: MaterialButton(
            color: Colors.red,
            onPressed: () {
              Navigator.push(
                  context, MaterialPageRoute(builder: (context) => ScreenC()));
            },
            child: const Text('navigate'),
            padding: const EdgeInsets.all(7),
          ),
        ));
  }
}

class ScreenC extends StatefulWidget {
  @override
  _ScreenC createState() => _ScreenC();
}

class _ScreenC extends State<ScreenC> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    print('Screen C built');
    return Scaffold(
      resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false,
      appBar: PreferredSize(
        preferredSize: const Size.fromHeight(70.312),
        child: AppBar(
          title: Text('Screen C'),
        ),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Container(
            alignment: Alignment.center,
            width: (123.725),
            height: (37),
            color: Colors.grey,
            child: TextFormField(
              decoration: const InputDecoration(
                  contentPadding: EdgeInsets.all(0),
                  border: InputBorder.none,
                  isDense: true,
                  hintText: "1"),
              style: TextStyle(
                fontSize: (16),
                fontWeight: FontWeight.w500,
                color: Colors.green[700],
              ),
              keyboardType: TextInputType.numberWithOptions(decimal: true),
              textAlign: TextAlign.center,
              onTap: () {},
              onChanged: (value) {},
              onEditingComplete: () {},
            )),
      ),
      backgroundColor: Colors.white,
    );
  }
}

```

I recently [ran into a bug caused by TextFormField's controller listener](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/143249#issuecomment-2040799516). I thought it might be related to this, but it seems that this bug is not specific to TextFormField, and TextField also causes it.
Is there any way to overcome this problem temporarily?
@bahag-chandrana do you have  any solutions to this problem so far ?

issues.title: [Android] Keyboard input failure on physical Android devices after screen lock/unlock
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Execute flutter run.
2. Focus TextField. Keyboard appears. Type something.
3. Lock android screen by pressing Power button.
4. Unlock android screen.
5. Type something again.

### Expected results

Keyboard input occurs in TextField

### Actual results

Keyboard input is not performed and is restored only after re-focusing on the TextField

This problem occurs only on physical devices.
List of devices on which this was noticed:
1) Readme 9A (Android 10, MIUI Global 12.0.17)
2) Nokia 5.3 (Android 11)
3) ZTE Smart 20 blade

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Scaffold(
      body: Center(child: TextField()),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

Hi @kensamare 
I can reproduce this issue with a simple `TextField` on my device `Realme 6, Android 11`.

The issue doesn't occur on iOS. Checking this on an Android native EditText sample, the issue also doesn't occur. 

I managed to work around the issue by following code, you can also try to see if it works. It will interfere with the TextField focus node through the app lifecycle state.

<details>
<summary>Sample code (workaround)</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  State<MyApp> createState() => _MyAppState();
}

class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> with WidgetsBindingObserver {
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    WidgetsBinding.instance.addObserver(this);
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    super.dispose();
    WidgetsBinding.instance.removeObserver(this);
  }

  @override
  void didChangeAppLifecycleState(AppLifecycleState state) {
    super.didChangeAppLifecycleState(state);
    if (state == AppLifecycleState.resumed) {
      _focusNode.requestFocus();
    } else if (state == AppLifecycleState.paused) {
      _focusNode.unfocus();
    }
  }

  final FocusNode _focusNode = FocusNode();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: TextField(
            focusNode: _focusNode,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

I started looking into this but the RealMe I was testing with no longer seems to be able to connect to my computer, and I don't believe we have another around so I won't be able to make any further progress on this for now.

Before that happened, I compared text input events between a Pixel 3A and the Realme X2 Pro and I noticed that the call to `InputConnectionAdaptor.closeConnection` ([source](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/94023d711db3f950a235fd84a80a3dde4dd86338/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java#L249)) which is an override of `BaseInputConnection.closeConnection` ([docs](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/BaseInputConnection#closeConnection())) is called twice on the Realme (once when the screen is locked and once when the screen is unlocked), whereas it is only called once when the screen is locked on the Pixel, so I wonder if it has something to do with that. According to [the docs of the parent class to `BaseInputConnection`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/InputConnection#closeConnection()), this method should only be called once. I wanted to test out limiting the number of times it is called temporarily to see if that would fix this issue but my phone stopped connecting before that.

Agreed that the deviceType should be direcitonalPad.

Hi @justinmc, I've made a little investigation and have some insights.

When `KeyEvent` occurs, its data is sent to the framework via the `flutter/keyevent` channel. The `getSource()` function of `KeyEvent` returns an `int` representation of the input event source. This value is sent as is without any modifications.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/961ce3ec82ab88237be012c24eee24f9b10591dd/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/embedding/engine/systemchannels/KeyEventChannel.java#L72

After that, the `RawKeyEventDataAndroid` is created from the received data.
The actual conversion from `int` to `KeyEventDeviceType` is happening in the `_convertDeviceType` function in `hardware_keyboard.dart`. The HEX constants are correct according to the docs: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/InputDevice.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/961ce3ec82ab88237be012c24eee24f9b10591dd/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart#L1201-L1226

The input source in the `int` form, which is getting received from the Android TV emulator when typing something on the directional pad, is `769 -> 0x00000301`. There is no input source with such a value in `_convertDeviceType`. This is the reason why `ui.KeyEventDeviceType.keyboard` is getting returned. There is no `SOURCE_X` defined with such a value in the docs either. So why is this happening?

It turns out that the `getSource()` function can return a combined value with multiple sources. So the value `0x00000301` is the combination of `SOURCE_KEYBOARD` and `SOURCE_DPAD`: `0x00000101 | 0x00000201 = 0x00000301`.

Next, I created the Android demo app to verify this:

```kotlin
package com.example.keyeventsapp

import android.os.Bundle
import android.util.Log
import android.view.InputDevice
import android.view.KeyEvent
import androidx.activity.enableEdgeToEdge
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
import androidx.core.view.ViewCompat
import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat


class MainActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        enableEdgeToEdge()
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main)
        ViewCompat.setOnApplyWindowInsetsListener(findViewById(R.id.main)) { v, insets ->
            val systemBars = insets.getInsets(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
            v.setPadding(systemBars.left, systemBars.top, systemBars.right, systemBars.bottom)
            insets
        }

        for (deviceId in InputDevice.getDeviceIds()) {
            val device = InputDevice.getDevice(deviceId)
            Log.d("event", "Device: ${device}")
        }
    }

    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
        Log.i("event", "source: ${event.source}")
        Log.i("event", "action: ${event.action}")
        Log.i("event", "keyCode: ${event.keyCode}")
        Log.i("event", "")
        return true;
    }
}
```

As you can see, input devices may have combined source values such as `0x301 ( keyboard dpad )` or `0x80005103 ( keyboard touchscreen stylus )`.

What this means for us:
1) There might be more than one source for `KeyEvent` (e.g., `SOURCE_KEYBOARD` and `SOURCE_DPAD`)
2) Since there might be more than one source, a simple equality check in `_convertDeviceType` is not enough. We have to do something like `(data.eventSource & 0x00000101) == 0x00000101` for each `SOURCE_X` constant to check whether `eventSource` contains a specific source.

With that being said, I am not sure how to proceed. The documentation for `KeyEvent.deviceType` says `The source device type for the key event.`. However, it turns out that we can't reliably determine whether it was received from the dpad or keyboard, as the `source` value contains both.

What are your thoughts on this?

issues.title: [iOS Web] EditableText's cursor position jumps to 0 after changing cursor position and entering some text when `readOnly=true`
comments: @danagbemava-nc - FYI

Hi @the-ack, I think there might have been a slight miscommunication in the original issue. In your steps to reproduce in the original issue, you outlined some issues by numbering them, those were the items that I was suggesting be broken into separate issues to make them easier to track. 

I think we should repurpose this issue to track point 2 from the initial report. What do you think?

> (Issue 2) Click / tap into the input box to move the cursor to a different location. Click a button to enter a character. The character will be entered at the cursor's current position. Then the cursor will jump back to position 0 and subsequent characters will always appear at position 0. Adding 'BBB' after the 'B' in 'A B A' becomes 'BBA BB A' instead of the expected 'A BBBB A'.
Hi @danagbemava-nc, I agree with your suggestion and have updated this issue to be specific to "Issue 2", manually moving the cursor position before inserting text.
Some other related issues can be found in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/103531 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/103520
Just wanted to add that this issue also appears in Chrome on iOS. Please add a "browser: chrome-ios" label. Thanks
Hi @the-ack, all browsers on iOS use the same engine as safari, IIRC, so chances are if an issue is on safari, the other browsers will experience it as well.
Thanks @danagbemava-nc for clarifying.

This issue is assigned to @mdebbar but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!
This issue was assigned to @mdebbar but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.
It looks like the cursor position was changed in response to a dom even because `DefaultTextEditingStrategy.handleChange` is at the bottom of the stacktrace.

It's a little strange that cursor positions can change at all, AFAIK there isn't a cursor change on native web inputs with the `readonly` attribute. 

However, the behavior is inconsistent between platforms, so it warrants further investigation.  Marking as a P2.

This seems to be a framework issue. We must be interpreting mouse events differently from touch events. It may be intentional and require customization to achieve what your app is trying to achieve. Removing the web label for now.

  Thank you for taking a look at it and responding quickly.

Hi @Dall127 we recently introduced device based dragging for scrolling in order to match platform expectations. On desktop by default now, dragging to scroll with a mouse is disabled. It is possible to enable it though, there is a migration guide available here: https://flutter.dev/docs/release/breaking-changes/default-scroll-behavior-drag
Please let me know if this resolves your issue.

Hi @Piinks, I tried switching to the beta channel (as the documentation specified it was not yet implemented on stable), and implementing the custom scroll behavior,  but it still is not working as expected. The mouse pointer is still staying stuck as the "text highlighting cursor"  (pictured below) over all of the widget, which is breaking any scrolling behavior from the pages widget. Though, when I initiate scrolling with the trackpad, it does allow me to scroll with the mouse for a split second before reverting back to the text highlighting cursor. My guess is the selectable text widget controls the mouse behavior as a rectangle as the entire size of the full widget, instead of smaller rectangles around the actual text.

![IMG_8509](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31700756/132136154-e997cdf1-7860-401d-8a08-bf13dc912c02.jpg)

> I tried switching to the beta channel (as the documentation specified it was not yet implemented on stable), and implementing the custom scroll behavior,

Hi @Dall127 can you share a code snippet of your custom scroll behavior? Also, I am not sure from your last comment what your expected behavior is. Can you also supply Expected/Actual in the context of your update to clarify? Thanks!

Ah, I see now, thank you @Dall127 for testing out the custom scroll behavior and reporting back! So it looks like there is a conflict of gestures here - selecting text and dragging.  @chunhtai and @justinmc may have some insight on SelectableText and how we might be able to configure this in the way you would like.
I am not sure this is a scrolling issue, it appears to really be an issue of gesture precedence.

@Dall127 Trying out your code in your original description up top, I see the following behavior:

  * I can change pages by scrolling horizontally (I'm using a Magic Mouse on Mac).
  * I cannot change pages by clicking and dragging horizontally with the mouse.

Also, if I remove the text altogether, I'm still not able to change pages by click and dragging horizontally.  I assume that's a deliberate choice to not support that on desktop.

Are you seeing the same behavior?

@justinmc, that is the behavior I'm seeing. And @darshankawar, I don't believe that ticket accurately resolves the issue being discussed on this ticket. I tested the code adopting the new default scrolling behavior, and the issue still persists (It in fact existed before the update to 2.5). I believe it is a problem of both the selectable text and page view gestures conflicting
Does the behavior actually change for you if you remove the SelectableText?
@justinmc I can confirm that if I use the 'Text' widget in place of the 'SelectableText' widget, it does create the desired behavior.

> Hi @Dall127 we recently introduced device based dragging for scrolling in order to match platform expectations. On desktop by default now, dragging to scroll with a mouse is disabled. It is possible to enable it though, there is a migration guide available here: https://flutter.dev/docs/release/breaking-changes/default-scroll-behavior-drag Please let me know if this resolves your issue.

And finally, I would assume that the mouse wheel would scroll the PageView, but it didn't work for me. Do you think that is because the PageView's scroll direction is set to Horizontal? It would be great if the mouse wheel could scroll the page view. Does the mouse wheel scroll a normal horizontal scrollable view?

Thanks in advance!

comments:
Hi @alihassan143 , thanks for the report and sharing stack trace.
However, without a code sample, it's nearly impossible for the team to investigate. Could you provide a minimal, runnable code sample? Also, please let us know if you are using any custom fonts or rendering specific languages/characters when this ANR happens.

@mbcorona We are still unable to consistently reproduce this on our end, as it appears to occur randomly across different devices. We’ve tried several troubleshooting techniques, but the issue persists.

Our app primarily handles large text inputs where users often paste anywhere from 10,000 to over 20,000 words. The performance degradation seems most prevalent when the word count exceeds the 20,000 mark. At this threshold, we encounter significant lagging on both physical Android devices and simulators, often leading to the app becoming unresponsive or crashing entirely.
HI @alihassan143 , I'll route this to team-text-input, knowing this issue is triggered when pasting 10,000–20,000+ words into a TextField for further investigation.

from text-input-triage: This is potentially related to existing issues https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/90063 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/96173. If those issues are the same as this issue then I think we should close this. Let me know if this is the case.

_This is the translated version of the original issue via google translate_


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set the `keyboardType` property to `TextInputType.number`.
2. Enter any content, and then keep switching the keyboard display state 
3. After repeating several times, the content of TextField disappears

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

The content is always displayed unless the content is manually cleared

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

The TextField with `keyboardType` is set, and the content disappears when the keyboard display state is switched

issues.title: [Web] In iOS PWA, various elements at the bottom of the screen do not respond to user actions after focusing TextField and double tapped
issues.body:

     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

After focus out the TextField and double tapped, bottom of the screen do not respond to user actions.
This causes various elements are not respond to tap. ( ex. Button, CheckBox…)
The issue is only occurred iOS PWA, and not on Android, iOS Safari(not PWA).
related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111896

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run -d chrome --release` on the code sample (To visualize the background area, run with removed Scaffold and `--release` added)
2. Focus on TextField and press the Done button to hide the software keyboard
3. Double tap at the bottom of screen
4. The blue background area is pushed up and the buttons covering the area are not respond (No ripple effects)
5. Double tap near the TextField
6. The blue background area is lowered and only buttons not included in that area respond to taps

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
After the TextField focus out, various elements at the bottom of the screen respond to user interaction.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
After the TextField focus out, various elements at the bottom of the screen **do not** respond to user interaction.

<details>
<summary>Code Sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Column(
        children: [
          const TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              hintText: 'Enter Something',
            ),
          ),
          const Spacer(),
          SizedBox(
            height: 200,
            child: Column(
              mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.spaceEvenly,
              children: [
                FractionallySizedBox(
                  widthFactor: 0.8,
                  child: ElevatedButton(
                    onPressed: () => {},
                    style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(
                      backgroundColor: Colors.green,
                    ),
                    child: const Text('button1'),
                  ),
                ),
                FractionallySizedBox(
                  widthFactor: 0.8,
                  child: ElevatedButton(
                    onPressed: () => {},
                    style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(
                      backgroundColor: Colors.green,
                    ),
                    child: const Text('button2'),
                  ),
                ),
              ],
            ),
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

issues.title: TextField with lots of text can hang app
comments: /cc @justinmc

Thanks for the reproducible issue, this has been in the back of my mind for awhile.  I want to figure out exactly what the bottleneck is here.  Just some quick thoughts for now:

  * This isn't really an insurmountable amount of text (~800kb back of the napkin?).
  * Currently, the entire contents of the field is synced between the framework and engine on every change, which is going to be a performance concern even if it's not exactly the cause of this slowdown here.
  * @Renzo-Olivares introduced the TextEditingDeltas API in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/88477, which may give us a way to work with deltas instead of the entire string.
flutter/engine#29036 may help for macOS.
@cbracken Has this problem been solved ?
@letungcntt -- @justinmc will be working on migrating Windows to a delta-based approach but this has not yet landed.

@letungcntt This is the PR you'll need: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/29036.  It was rolled into the framework in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/93941 in 2.9.0-0.1.pre, but it might have been reverted and rolled in again later...  Anyway it seems like your version 2.9.0 is slightly too old.  Master definitely has it.

Also, I have to point out that this is all still early and experimental and not currently a viable fix for this issue.  Using `enableDeltaModel = true` will completely break EditableText and TextField, etc.  Currently this feature is really only for those who are implementing their own low-level text editors.  Lastly, we currently send the old state of the field along with the delta on each change ([oldText docs](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextEditingDelta/oldText.html)), so there is no memory improvement unless we remove this.

Tldr; It's very early for this feature.  You won't be able to improve performance for large amounts of text unless you are ready to really hack text editing on your own, and even then nothing is guaranteed to improve.

Edit: Here is an editor I've used as a demo for deltas to give you an idea of how it could be used: https://github.com/justinmc/delta-text-editor-demo

Hi Everybody, as I understand the PR has since been merged. Still I can not get it to work on windows, even when setting the option `enableDeltaModel: true` I still only receive calls to `updateEditingValue`. Should this be working? I can post an mwp here if you want.

@PaulEibensteiner Are you doing this while using your own implementation of `DeltaTextInputClient` or are you using Flutter's included `EditableText`/`TextField`? If it's the latter you would have to change `implements TextInputClient` to `implements DeltaTextInputClient` and define `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` accordingly. Currently Flutter does not provide a `DeltaTextInputClient` implementation. However this will not fix the performance issue because deltas currently are only sent one way from the `engine` to the `framework` and not vice versa. There is still more work to be done around this issue.
I am using my own implementation, something along the lines of [this](https://gist.github.com/PaulEibensteiner/d934f1751ab0c4ba2ee0a31b1c9c69e5), which does not work for me as explained above.

Btw, for a custom more performant text field, do you think it would be possible to simply set the current text editing value only to the paragraph(s) currently being edited via `TextInputConnection.setEditingState`? It seems to me that this should prevent the TextInputConnection from sending the whole document back and forth.

What platform/flutter version are you running on? That sounds like it could be part of a solution, though this must also be handled on the `TextInputPlugin` for the platform on the engine side. Also there is still work to be done on the performance of laying out large amounts of text. See: #92173

sorry forgot about that

Actually, I do not keep the paragraphs as text spans in memory, i only store strings of formatting glyphs so I don't think that #92173 actually applies to me. Tbo I currently do not have problems with speed either, I just wondered why the texEditingDeltas would not work. Still thank you though!
I think the engine that comes with Flutter 2.10.4 stable doesn't yet support deltas on all platforms. The master branch currently supports all platforms.
Oh I'm sorry I didn't realize it was merged so recently
TextInput.setEditingState for a large text is still expensive, will have a delta api in the future?
@Renzo-Olivares @justinmc how much work remains for text editing delta support on desktop? Do we have documentation on how users can adopt it?
Have we also considered moving away from the JSON codec for text?

@dnfield I recently realized that was where we were moving, makes sense to me but there are not concrete plans.  Is there an overall issue somewhere for migrating away from JSON or should I create one for text input specifically?

I don't think we can safely drop the JSON codec without some major ecosystem migration. A specific one for text input would be good if we don't have it.

It might also be good to have an umbrella issue to drop the JSON codec usage within the framework, although I think some of the usages that are left outside of text are channels that tend to have small and rarely used messages.

@Renzo-Olivares Thanks for `TextInputClient/DeltaTextInputClient`, it solved my problem. I believe`TextInput.setEditingState` should have a delta api, which can significantly improve performance on a large text. A basic design, selection and text should be separated when sending to platforms. The current situation is that any change will send both of them to platforms, even if I just changed the selection offset.

**EDIT:** Actually, I realized that for my `onKey` callback function to be called, I do not need to press a key of the keyboard first. The keyboard just has to **open** at least once. Knowing this, I used a somewhat ugly workaround to :

1. Open the keyboard by autofocusing on a `TextField` widget.
2. And then request the focus back to the `Focus` widget (automatically closing the keyboard as well).

```dart
final keyNode = FocusNode();
final textNode = FocusNode();
textNode.addListener(() => {if (textNode.hasFocus) keyNode.requestFocus()});

...

Visibility.maintain(
  visible: false,
  child: Focus(
    focusNode: keyNode,
    onKey: (data, event) { /* callback function */ },
    child: TextField(
      focusNode: textNode,
      autofocus: true,
    ),
  ),
),
```

Hello, there's an issue with flutter 2.10.5 when opening keyboard and using `responsive_builder`. It will call `build` and `didChangeDependencies` multiple times. This is not happening in 2.8.1. 

**2.8 video below**:

https://github.com/marctan/demo_rebuild

We are also experiencing this issue in production with Flutter 3.38.5.

**Environment:**
- Flutter 3.38.5 (latest stable)
- Syncfusion DataGrid 31.2.18
- Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Android Chrome)

**Reproduction:**
1. Open a Flutter web app on a mobile browser
2. Open a Syncfusion DataGrid filter dialog
3. Tap on the search TextField inside the filter popup
4. The keyboard opens, causing multiple rebuilds
5. The TextField loses focus immediately
6. User cannot type anything - every tap causes the keyboard to open and close

**Impact:**
